note: Annotated term sheets for learning. The first three are realistic composites grounded in docs/research-us-ppa.md; the two marked 'Real:' annotate actual executed contracts filed publicly with the SEC (linked in realDocs). Each clause's glossaryRefs must match a term in glossary.json exactly so the UI can link them.

examples.title: Corporate Virtual PPA (VPPA)
examples.summary: The most common corporate structure: a purely financial contract-for-differences on a solar or wind project, with the RECs delivered to the buyer. No electrons reach the buyer's site.

clauses.label: Structure
clauses.value: Virtual (financial) PPA — contract for differences
clauses.annotation: No physical delivery. The parties settle the gap between a fixed strike and the floating market price; economically a fixed-for-floating swap.

clauses.label: Facility
clauses.value: 200 MW solar, ERCOT (West Texas)
clauses.annotation: The generator's location sets the node where it physically earns revenue — the root of basis risk if the deal settles elsewhere.

clauses.label: Term
clauses.value: 12 years from Commercial Operation Date
clauses.annotation: Corporate buyers increasingly favour shorter terms (10–15 yrs) than the traditional ~20-year utility PPA; the term must still be long enough to finance the project.

clauses.label: Fixed (strike) price
clauses.value: $42.00 / MWh, flat for the term
clauses.annotation: The price the buyer effectively locks in. Under a flat/fixed structure the buyer bears the downside if market prices fall below the strike.

clauses.label: Settlement
clauses.value: Monthly financial settlement; Floating = real-time LMP
clauses.annotation: Each month: settlement = (LMP − strike) × volume. Market above strike, generator pays buyer; below, buyer pays generator.

clauses.label: Settlement location
clauses.value: ERCOT North Hub
clauses.annotation: Buyers usually push to settle at the less-volatile hub. That leaves the developer absorbing the node-to-hub gap — the single most important risk term to negotiate.

clauses.label: Environmental attributes
clauses.value: 100% of RECs conveyed to Buyer
clauses.annotation: The RECs are what the fixed price really buys — they back the buyer's clean-energy claim. One REC per MWh generated.

clauses.label: Volume
clauses.value: As-generated (unit-contingent)
clauses.annotation: Buyer takes the project's actual output, so it carries volume and shape risk — generation may not line up with high-price hours.

clauses.label: Credit support
clauses.value: Seller letter of credit; Buyer parent guarantee
clauses.annotation: Corporate VPPAs are often two-way, unlike one-way utility PPAs — each side secures its exposure if prices move against it.

clauses.label: Change in law
clauses.value: Allocated; tax-credit risk to Seller
clauses.annotation: States who absorbs a law or tax change that hurts the economics — material given how much value sits in IRA tax credits.

examples.title: Utility-scale physical PPA
examples.summary: A generator physically delivers power and title to a utility offtaker at a defined point, typically under a long, investment-grade-backed contract that banks can finance.

realDocs.label: EEI Master Power Purchase & Sale Agreement

clauses.label: Structure
clauses.value: Physical PPA — energy + title delivered
clauses.annotation: Electrons and legal title pass to the offtaker at the delivery point; this is a sale of power, not a financial hedge.

clauses.label: Term
clauses.value: 20 years from COD
clauses.annotation: The traditional utility tenor — long enough to amortize project debt and give the sponsor a return.

clauses.label: Price
clauses.value: $38 / MWh, escalating 2% per year
clauses.annotation: An escalating structure: the buyer commits to a rising price path and bears price risk if the market falls below it.

clauses.label: Delivery point
clauses.value: Project busbar / interconnection node
clauses.annotation: Where title transfers and where the price is referenced — defines who bears congestion between the node and any further sink.

clauses.label: Environmental attributes
clauses.value: Bundled — RECs delivered with the energy
clauses.annotation: A 'bundled' deal conveys both the power and its RECs, versus a VPPA where only the financial value and RECs move.

clauses.label: Guaranteed COD / delay damages
clauses.value: Target COD with daily delay damages
clauses.annotation: Penalizes the seller for missing the in-service date — protects the buyer's expected supply.

clauses.label: Capacity buydown
clauses.value: Price/payment adjustment if under-built
clauses.annotation: If the project comes in smaller than promised, the seller compensates the buyer.

clauses.label: Curtailment
clauses.value: Deemed-generation payment for buyer/grid curtailment
clauses.annotation: When output can't be taken, the seller is paid on a 'deemed generated' basis — but compensation often excludes transmission causes outside the offtaker's control.

clauses.label: Credit support
clauses.value: Seller posts 6–18 months of payments (one-way)
clauses.annotation: Classic one-way utility security: the seller posts an LC or guarantee; the investment-grade utility usually posts nothing.

clauses.label: Force majeure
clauses.value: Mutual excuse; insurance-backed
clauses.annotation: Excuses performance for events beyond control; how loss lands depends largely on insurance availability.

examples.title: Data-center nuclear restart PPA
examples.summary: A hyperscaler underwrites the restart of an existing nuclear unit with a long firm offtake — the structure behind the Microsoft × Constellation Three Mile Island deal.

clauses.label: Structure
clauses.value: Long-term offtake of a restarted nuclear unit
clauses.annotation: The buyer's 20-year commitment underwrites the capex to recommission a retired unit — trading tenor for speed-to-firm-power.

clauses.label: Capacity
clauses.value: ~835 MW firm baseload
clauses.annotation: Large, around-the-clock output — what an AI data center needs, and what variable solar/wind alone can't supply.

clauses.label: Term
clauses.value: 20 years
clauses.annotation: Long tenor gives the generator the revenue certainty to justify the restart investment.

clauses.label: Conditions precedent
clauses.value: NRC approval; restart capex; grid studies
clauses.annotation: Delivery is gated on regulatory and engineering milestones — nuclear restarts take years and federal sign-off.

clauses.label: Delivery / matching
clauses.value: Grid-connected; matched in the PJM region
clauses.annotation: Power flows to the grid (front-of-meter), and the buyer matches it against its regional data-center load.

clauses.label: Environmental attributes
clauses.value: Carbon-free attributes to Buyer
clauses.annotation: Nuclear output is carbon-free, supporting the buyer's clean-energy and 24/7 CFE goals.

clauses.label: Credit support
clauses.value: Investment-grade buyer covenant
clauses.annotation: A creditworthy hyperscaler offtaker is exactly what makes a project like this financeable.

examples.title: Real: EPE × NM SunTower solar PPA (2008)
examples.summary: An actual executed 92 MW solar PPA between New Mexico SunTower, LLC and El Paso Electric, signed October 17, 2008 — public because EPE filed it with the SEC as a material contract (Exhibit 10.07). Every value below is quoted from the real document.

realDocs.label: Full contract text — SEC EDGAR (EPE Exhibit 10.07)

clauses.label: Parties & why it's public
clauses.value: New Mexico SunTower, LLC (seller) × El Paso Electric Company (buyer), Oct 17, 2008
clauses.annotation: Real PPAs surface publicly when a listed company files them as material contracts with the SEC, or a utility files them with its state commission. EDGAR full-text search for 'power purchase agreement' in Exhibit 10 filings is the best free library of executed deals.

clauses.label: Facility
clauses.value: Solar 'SunTower' facility, Designed Maximum Output 92 MW, New Mexico — two prospective sites at execution (Exhibit B)
clauses.annotation: Signed before the final site was even chosen: the contract lets the seller pick between two candidate sites before the financial-commitment milestone. Real deals are signed years ahead of steel in the ground.

clauses.label: Term
clauses.value: Through the last day of the 20th Commercial Operation Year (Art. 2)
clauses.annotation: The classic 20-year utility tenor — long enough to amortize project debt. Note the term runs from commercial operation, not signature: add the multi-year build and the total relationship approaches 25 years.

clauses.label: Price
clauses.value: **** (redacted — confidential treatment requested)
clauses.annotation: The most-studied number in any PPA is the one you usually can't see: the SEC granted confidential treatment and the price appears as asterisks. Commercial price terms are the norm to redact; structure, security, and risk clauses stay visible.

clauses.label: Environmental attributes
clauses.value: All RECs transferred to and owned by EPE at no additional cost (recitals); seller bears REC compliance admin costs up to $25,000/yr under NMPRC Rule 572
clauses.annotation: A bundled deal: energy and RECs move together for one price, and the contract even allocates the paperwork cost of REC registration. New Mexico's commission rules are baked directly into the contract.

clauses.label: Point of Delivery
clauses.value: Point at which electricity is delivered to EPE, adjusted for metering losses; generally the same as the Point of Interconnection
clauses.annotation: Delivery at the project's own interconnection means the buyer takes the power where the plant sits — the utility, not the seller, bears congestion beyond that point. Compare a hub-settled VPPA, where the developer absorbs the node-to-hub gap.

clauses.label: Development security
clauses.value: Security Fund of $20/kW of Designed Maximum Output within 30 days of execution, rising to $40/kW at permits/interconnection agreement (no later than June 1, 2010)
clauses.annotation: Security ramps with project risk: a small stake at signature, doubled once permits and the interconnection agreement make the project real. Sized per kW, so ~$1.8M rising to ~$3.7M for a 92 MW plant.

clauses.label: Letter-of-credit standard
clauses.value: LC issuer must hold an unsecured bond rating of A− or better from both S&P and Moody's (§11.1(C)(1))
clauses.annotation: Credit support is only as good as the bank behind it — the contract specifies dual-agency minimum ratings for the issuer, written in 2008 as Lehman was proving exactly why.

clauses.label: Delivery excuse / curtailment
clauses.value: 'Delivery Excuse' covers events solely due to EPE's actions; carve-out for temporary disconnection by the interconnection provider
clauses.annotation: Who-caused-it drives who pays: buyer-caused curtailment excuses the seller (and typically still pays), while grid-operator disconnections sit in their own carve-out — the same cause-based allocation taught in the curtailment clause pattern.

clauses.label: Commissioning
clauses.value: Test Date no later than 45 days before COD; commissioning tests and conditions to commercial operation (Art. 4)
clauses.annotation: COD isn't declared, it's proven: the plant must pass defined commissioning tests before the 20-year clock and full payments start. Test energy before COD is typically bought at a discounted rate.

examples.title: Real: SCPPA × Milford Wind II prepaid PPA (2010)
examples.type: Real executed contract / prepaid municipal wind
examples.summary: An actual executed 102 MW wind PPA between the Southern California Public Power Authority and Milford Wind Corridor Phase II, LLC (First Wind), dated March 1, 2010 — a rare public example of a PREPAID structure, with real liquidated-damages and security numbers.

realDocs.label: Full contract text — SEC EDGAR (First Wind Exhibit 10.57)

clauses.label: Parties & origination
clauses.value: SCPPA (joint powers authority of SoCal municipal utilities) × Milford Wind Corridor Phase II, LLC; RFP issued Sept 1, 2006 — executed March 1, 2010
clauses.annotation: Three and a half years from RFP to signature — a real data point for the origination-to-execution timeline the lifecycle section describes.

clauses.label: Structure — prepaid
clauses.value: Buyer pays a lump-sum Prepayment Amount for 'Guaranteed Generation' over the term (§7.1(g)); excess energy priced separately
clauses.annotation: The rare prepaid PPA: a municipal buyer can raise tax-exempt bonds cheaply, prepay for 20 years of output, and capture a lower effective price. The seller gets construction-scale capital day one — but the buyer now carries delivery risk, which is why the guarantee and security package below is so heavy.

clauses.label: Facility
clauses.value: Nominal 102 MW wind facility near Milford, Utah
clauses.annotation: Phase II of the Milford Wind Corridor — an expansion PPA next to an operating Phase I, which is exactly the de-risked posture buyers prefer.

clauses.label: Term
clauses.value: Delivery Term: 20 years from the Prepayment Date (§2.2)
clauses.annotation: The clock runs from the prepayment, not COD — because the buyer's money is in from that date. Term mechanics always follow the money.

clauses.label: Price
clauses.value: Prepaid Energy Price: $47.00/MWh (definitions); Excess Energy per Appendix A
clauses.annotation: A real 2010 wind price, visible because this filer didn't redact it: $47/MWh. Berkeley Lab's price indices put 2018–19 utility solar below $30/MWh — a decade of cost decline in one comparison.

clauses.label: Point of Delivery
clauses.value: Seller's interconnection facilities at the Intermountain Power Project 345-kV switchyard
clauses.annotation: The project sits in Utah; the buyers' load is in Southern California. Delivery at the IPP switchyard puts the power onto the existing high-voltage DC line to Los Angeles — the transmission path is the deal's hidden foundation.

clauses.label: Delay liquidated damages
clauses.value: $5,000/day for the first 30 days past the Guaranteed COD, then $19,375/day, capped at $4,800,000 (§3.5(a))
clauses.annotation: Real delay-damage numbers: gentle for the first month, then nearly 4× — designed to sting for slippage the seller can control while capping total exposure at a financeable number.

clauses.label: Credit support
clauses.value: LD Security LC of $4,800,000 + separate Performance Security LC of $5,000,000, each from a 'Qualified Issuer' (Appendix M forms)
clauses.annotation: Two stacked letters of credit securing different obligations — one sized exactly to the LD cap, one for ongoing performance. Note the contract even prescribes the LC form as an appendix.

clauses.label: Curtailment — deemed delivered
clauses.value: Curtailed energy under §5.6(g) is deemed Delivered Energy toward the Guaranteed Annual Quantity
clauses.annotation: Because the buyer prepaid for guaranteed output, curtailed energy still counts as delivered for the guarantee — the prepaid structure flips the usual curtailment fight from 'who pays' to 'what counts.'

clauses.label: Environmental attributes & capacity rights
clauses.value: Sweeping 'Environmental Attributes' definition (all current or future credits/benefits) plus separate 'Capacity Rights' conveyed
clauses.annotation: The definition reaches attributes that don't exist yet ('current or future') — drafted so any later-created environmental product belongs to the buyer. Capacity value is conveyed as its own defined right.

clauses.label: Buyer options
clauses.value: Early-termination events and buyer purchase options over the facility (§2.4–2.5)
clauses.annotation: Municipal buyers often negotiate an option to buy the plant outright — the PPA doubles as a path to ownership, another feature the prepayment structure makes natural.
